name: Cosmo from La Barosa - circular by the Burbia and Sil rivers
name_original: Cosmo desde La Barosa – circular por los ríos Burbia y Sil
region_intro: Road cycling around Carucedo offers routes through a landscape shaped by ancient Roman gold mining, featuring dramatic reddish clay formations of Las Médulas and the tranquil Lake Carucedo. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including extensive chestnut forests and areas with significant elevation changes. Cyclists can explore routes that incorporate historical sites and natural features, providing diverse riding experiences. Best road cycling routes around Carucedo The most popular road cycling route is Ponferrada and the Bierzo Valley, an easy 27.2 miles (43.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This route features gentle gradients through the Bierzo Valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cúa River in Cacabelos – Secondary road loop from Toral de los Vados, a moderate 44.8 miles (72.1 km) path. This route follows secondary roads alongside the Cúa River, offering scenic views of the surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the O Cebreiro – Cebreiro Pass loop from Toral de los Vados, a 52.0 miles (83.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Carucedo is defined by the unique Roman mining landscape of Las Médulas, the serene Lake Carucedo, and extensive chestnut forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy valley rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Carucedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from 5 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Carucedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Carucedo?
A: Carucedo offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 40 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 4 easy, 14 moderate, and 24 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Carucedo?
A: Road cycling around Carucedo features diverse terrain. You'll encounter routes through the dramatic, reddish clay formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4612988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Médulas&lt;/a&gt;, tranquil areas around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5550699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Carucedo&lt;/a&gt;, and extensive chestnut forests. The region includes both gentle gradients in valleys and areas with significant elevation changes for more challenging rides.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for families in Carucedo?
A: Yes, Carucedo offers easy road cycling routes that are suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046837904/ponferrada-and-the-bierzo-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponferrada and the Bierzo Valley&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy 43.9 km trail featuring gentle gradients, perfect for a relaxed family ride.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling climbs near Carucedo?
A: For challenging climbs, consider routes that venture into the more mountainous terrain surrounding Carucedo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40329746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Cebreiro – Cebreiro Pass loop from Toral de los Vados&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 83.7 km trail that leads through significant ascents, often completed in about 4 hours 12 minutes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that offer scenic viewpoints of Las Médulas?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area incorporate the stunning landscapes of Las Médulas. While cycling, you can often catch glimpses of the ancient Roman gold mine. For panoramic vistas, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3444048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Orellán&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular views of the area.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the Carucedo area?
A: Yes, there are several circular road cycling routes available. An example of a challenging circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046247431/cosmo-from-la-barosa-circular-by-the-burbia-and-sil-rivers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cosmo from La Barosa - circular by the Burbia and Sil rivers&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 65 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What are some interesting historical sites or natural attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in attractions. Along your rides, you can explore the ancient Roman gold mines of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4612988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Médulas&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3444048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellán Galleries&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3444033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Encantada Cave and La Cuevona&lt;/a&gt;. The serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5550699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago de Carucedo&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent natural feature you might encounter.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Carucedo?
A: Carucedo is an excellent destination for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially with the changing colors of the chestnut forests. Summer can also be enjoyable, though it's advisable to ride earlier in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there options for parking when starting a road cycling route in Carucedo?
A: Yes, Carucedo and its surrounding villages typically offer parking options. Many routes start from towns like Toral de los Vados or near key attractions, where you can usually find suitable places to park your vehicle before beginning your ride.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes with cafe stops in Carucedo?
A: Many road cycling routes in Carucedo pass through or near charming villages where you can find cafes and bars for a refreshing stop. The region is known for its rural accommodation and local eateries, providing opportunities to refuel and relax during your ride.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Carucedo?
A: The road cycling routes in Carucedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique historical landscapes, the varied terrain, and the opportunity to explore both challenging climbs and scenic, tranquil paths.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling options from Carucedo?
A: Yes, Carucedo serves as a great starting point for longer road cycling tours. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40330069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trabadelo Village – Church of the Conception loop from Toral de los Vados&lt;/a&gt;, at 88.3 km, offer a substantial distance for those looking for a longer day out on the bike.

Q: How can I download GPX tracks for road cycling routes in Carucedo?
A: For most routes on komoot, including those around Carucedo, you can easily download the GPX track directly from the route page. Simply navigate to the specific route you're interested in, and you'll find an option to download the GPX file for use with your cycling computer or navigation device.
tips: Spectacular views of all Galicia from the Ports of Gallegos. One of the best-known mountain passes of El Bierzo at 1,066 meters above sea level.
Corullón is a small town on top of a hill near the Burbia River. The most outstanding monuments are the castle of Corullón from the 15th century and the Church of San Esteban.

The Burbia river is located in the Leonese region of El Bierzo. It begins at an altitude of 1,000 meters above sea level and is one of the tributaries of the Sil River. It became famous for the Battle of the Burbia River in the year 791 between the Kingdom of Asturias and the Emirate of Córdoba.
